content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 1, 1990

Samuel Siegel
City Attorney
City of Pico Rivera
6615 Passons Boulevard
Pico Rivera, CA 90660

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-604

Dear Mr. Siegel:

This letter is in response to your request for confirmation
of the telephone advice provided to you on September 19, 1990,
regarding the duties and responsibilities of members of the Pico
Rivera Redevelopment Agency under the conflict-of-interest provi-
sions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

After a careful review of the information you have provided
to us, we conclude that with respect to the real property
interests held by Mr. Rick Mercado, Mr. John Chavez, Mr. Al
Natividad, and Mr. Garth Gardner, your letter accurately reflects
our advice. However, with respect to Mr. Patronite's residence,
we conclude that the public generally exception does not apply.

As you correctly state in your request for advice, Mr. Garth
Gardner, who owns a 141 unit apartment building within the
proposed amended project area, must abstain from participating in
decisions relating to the proposed amendment because his financial
interests will be affected materially by these decisions.

Conversely, Mr. Rick Mercado, Mr. John Chavez, and Mr. Al
Natividad, who own single family residences within 300 feet of the
proposed expanded project area, may participate in decisions
regarding amendments to the project area. This is so because
their interests in real property will be affected in a manner


1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-90-604
Page 2

which is not distinguishable from the effect of the decisions on
the public generally.

Finally, Mr. James Patronite must disqualify himself from
participating in decisions regarding amendments to the redevelopment plan because it would appear from the facts presented that
his interests will be affected differently from those of the
public generally. The financial effect of a decision on a public
official's interests is distinguishable from the effect on the
public generally unless the decision will affect the public
official's interests in substantially the same manner as it will
affect all members of the public or a significant segment of the
public. (Regulation 18707; In re Legan (1985) 9 FPPC Ops. 1; In
re Owen (1976) 2 FPPC Ops. 77, copies enclosed.) The "public
generally" is comprised of the entire jurisdiction of the agency.

Mr. Patronite's residence is situated on a lot which is approximately 20,000 square feet in area. We have previously
advised that the anticipated result of a redevelopment plan is an
increase in property values. (Brown Advice Letter, I-89-547, copy
enclosed.) In general, redevelopment decisions tend to have a
greater effect on the value of larger lots.

According to the information you have provided to us, approximately ten-percent of the residents in the jurisdiction own
homes erected on lots of 15,000 square feet or larger. For
purposes of the public generally exception we look at the number
of residences which are similarly situated to the public
official's residence. Clearly the number of residences which are
similarly situated to Mr. Patronite's residence cannot be greater
than ten-percent of the residences in the jurisdiction. This
group of residences is not sufficiently large to constitute a
significant segment of the public. Thus, Mr. Patronite may not
participate in decisions regarding amendments to the redevelopment
plan.

We trust this letter provides you with the guidance you have
requested. Should you have any further questions regarding this
matter, do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Blanca M. Breeze
    Counsel, Legal Division
